title: Sarga 56
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “Having heard Rama's words, Lakshmana, the destroyer of enemy heroes, spoke with folded hands to the radiant Rama.” through “O Lord, O Lord of the worlds, O great god, I have attained a bodiless state due to Nimi's curse, and you yourself are born from an egg and a…” through “At that very time, Mitra also performed the role of Varuna, accompanied by the Ocean of Milk and being worshipped by the best of gods.”

Themes: devotion, freedom from attachment. The passage moves from “At that very time, Urvashi, the foremost Apsara, arrived at that place by chance, accompanied by her friends.” through “Having heard those well-spoken words of Varuna, the protector of the world, Urvashi was extremely pleased and spoke.” through “Due to this misdeed, you, sullied by my wrath, will reside in the human world for some time.”

Themes: mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Go today to Pururavas, the royal sage, son of Budha and king of Kashi, O ill-minded one, for he will be your husband.” through “Then to him was born a glorious and very powerful son named Ayus, whose son, Nahusha, became one with splendor equal to Indra.” through “That Urvashi, with beautiful teeth, eyes, and eyebrows, then went to the earth due to that curse, resided there for many years, and returned to…”

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य भाषितं श्रुत्वा लक्ष्मणः परवीरहा । उवाच प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं राघवं दीप्ततेजसम् ।। 7.56.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Rama's words, Lakshmana, the destroyer of enemy heroes, spoke with folded hands to the radiant Rama.

verses.sanskrit: निक्षिप्तदेहौ काकुत्स्य कथं तौ द्विजपार्थिवौ । पुनर्देहेन संयोगं जग्मतुर्देवसम्मतौ ।। 7.56.2 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, how did those two, the Brahmin and the king, who had cast off their bodies and were honored by the gods, attain union with a body again?

verses.sanskrit: लक्ष्मणेनैवमुक्तस्तु रामश्चेक्ष्वाकुनन्दनः । प्रत्युवाच महातेजा लक्ष्मणं पुरुषर्षभः ।। 7.56.3 ।।
verses.translation: Rama, the greatly effulgent best among men and descendant of Ikshvaku, thus replied to Lakshmana who had spoken to him.

verses.sanskrit: तौ परस्परशापेन देहावुत्सृज्य धार्मिकौ । अभूतां नृपविप्रर्षी वायुभूतौ तपोधनौ ।। 7.56.4 ।।
verses.translation: Those two righteous ones, the king and the brahmin sage, having abandoned their bodies due to a mutual curse, became beings of air, their wealth being penance.

verses.sanskrit: अशरीरः शरीरस्य कृते ऽन्यस्य महामुनिः । वसिष्ठः सुमहातेजा जगाम पितुरन्तिकम् ।। 7.56.5 ।।
verses.translation: The great sage Vasishtha, exceedingly greatly effulgent, being bodiless, went to his father's presence for the sake of another body.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽभिवाद्य ततः पादौ देवदेवस्य धर्मवित् । पितामहमथोवाच वायुभूत इदं वचः ।। 7.56.6 ।।
verses.translation: Then, he, the knower of dharma, having become air, saluted the feet of the god of gods, his grandfather (Brahma), and spoke these words.

verses.sanskrit: भगवन्निमिशापेन विदेहत्वमुपागमम् । लोकनाथ महादेव अण्डजो ऽपि त्वमब्जजः ।। 7.56.7 ।।
verses.translation: O Lord, O Lord of the worlds, O great god, I have attained a bodiless state due to Nimi's curse, and you yourself are born from an egg and a lotus.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वेषां देहहीनानां महद्दुःखं भविष्यति । लुप्यन्ते सर्वकार्यणि हीनदेहस्य वै प्रभो । देहस्यान्यस्य सद्भावे प्रसादं कर्तुमर्हसि ।। 7.56.8 ।।
verses.translation: O Lord, great sorrow will befall all those without a body, as all actions are lost for one who lacks a physical form; therefore, you ought to grant the favor of another body.

verses.sanskrit: तमुवाच ततो ब्रह्मा स्वयम्भूरमितप्रभः । मित्रावरुणजं तेज प्रविश त्वं महायशः ।। 7.56.9 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Brahma, the self-born one of immeasurable splendor, said to him, "O greatly renowned one, enter the essence born of Mitra and Varuna."

verses.sanskrit: अयोनिजस्त्वं भविता तत्रापि द्विजसत्तम । धर्मेण महता युक्तः पुनरेष्यसि मे वशम् ।। 7.56.10 ।।
verses.translation: O best among twice-born, even there you will be born without a womb, and endowed with great righteousness, you will again return to my control.

verses.sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तु देवेन चाभिवाद्य प्रदक्षिणम् । कृत्वा पितामहं तूर्णं प्रययौ वरुणालयम् ।। 7.56.11 ।।
verses.translation: Thus spoken to by the god, and having saluted and circumambulated the grandfather Brahma, he quickly went to the abode of Varuna.

verses.sanskrit: तमेव कालं मित्रो ऽपि वरुणत्वमकारयत् । क्षीरोदेन सहोपेतः पूज्यमानः सुरोत्तमैः ।। 7.56.12 ।।
verses.translation: At that very time, Mitra also performed the role of Varuna, accompanied by the Ocean of Milk and being worshipped by the best of gods.

verses.sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु उर्वशी परमाप्सराः । यदृच्छया तमुद्देशमाययौ सखिभिर्वृता ।। 7.56.13 ।।
verses.translation: At that very time, Urvashi, the foremost Apsara, arrived at that place by chance, accompanied by her friends.

verses.sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा रूपसम्पन्नां क्रीडन्तीं वरुणालये । आविशत्परमो हर्षो वरुणं चोर्वशीकृते ।। 7.56.14 ।।
verses.translation: Having seen her, beautiful and playing in Varuna's abode, great joy entered Varuna because of Urvashi.

verses.sanskrit: स तां पद्मपलाशाक्षीं पूर्णचन्द्रनिभाननाम् । वरुणो वरयामास मैथुनायाप्सरोवराम् ।। 7.56.15 ।।
verses.translation: Varuna, seeing her with lotus-petal eyes and a face like the full moon, desired her, the best of Apsaras, for union.

verses.sanskrit: प्रत्युवाच ततः सा तु वरुणं प्राञ्जलिः स्थिता । मित्रेणाहं वृता साक्षात्पूर्वमेव सुरेश्वर ।। 7.56.16 ।।
verses.translation: Then she, standing with folded hands, replied to Varuna, saying, "O Lord of gods, I have already been directly chosen by Mitra."

verses.sanskrit: वरुणस्त्वब्रवीद्वाक्यं कन्दर्पशरपीडितः । इदं तेजः समुत्स्रक्ष्ये कुम्भे ऽस्मिन्देवनिर्मिते ।। 7.56.17 ।।
verses.translation: Tormented by the arrows of Kama, Varuna then spoke, saying, "I shall discharge this semen into this pot made by the gods."

verses.sanskrit: एवमुत्सृज्य सुश्रोणि त्वय्यहं वरवर्णिनि । कृतकामो भविष्यामि यदि नेच्छसि सङ्गमम् ।। 7.56.18 ।।
verses.translation: O beautiful-hipped lady of excellent complexion, if you do not desire union, I shall thus abandon my desire for you and consider my wish fulfilled.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य तल्लोकपालस्य वरुणस्य सुभाषितम् । उर्वशी परमप्रीता श्रुत्वा वाक्यमुवाच ह ।। 7.56.19 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard those well-spoken words of Varuna, the protector of the world, Urvashi was extremely pleased and spoke.

verses.sanskrit: काममेतद्भवत्वेवं हृदयं मे त्वयि स्थितम् । भावश्चाप्यधिकस्तुभ्यं देहो मित्रस्य तु प्रभो ।। 7.56.20 ।।
verses.translation: O lord, let it be so; my heart is indeed fixed on you, and my affection for you is great, but my body belongs to Mitra.

verses.sanskrit: उर्वश्या एवमुक्तस्तु रेतस्तन्महदद्भुतम् । ज्वलदग्निशिखाप्रख्यं तस्मिन्कुम्भे ह्यपासृजत् ।। 7.56.21 ।।
verses.translation: Thus addressed by Urvashi, he indeed discharged that great and wonderful semen, resembling a burning flame of fire, into that pot.

verses.sanskrit: उर्वशी त्वगमत्तत्र मित्रो वै यत्र देवता । तां तु मित्रः सुसङ्क्रम्य उर्वशीमिदमब्रवीत् ।। 7.56.22 ।।
verses.translation: But Urvashi went to the place where the deity Mitra was, and Mitra, having approached her, spoke these words to Urvashi.

verses.sanskrit: मया निमन्त्रिता पूर्वं कस्मात्त्वमवसर्जिता । पतिमन्यं वृतवती तस्मात्त्वं दुष्टचारिणी ।। 7.56.23 ।।
verses.translation: Why did you abandon me, whom I had previously invited, and choose another husband; therefore, you are an ill-behaved woman.

verses.sanskrit: अनेन दुष्कृतेन त्वं मत्क्रोधकलुषीकृता । मनुष्यलोकमास्थाय कञ्चित्कालं निवत्स्यसि ।। 7.56.24 ।।
verses.translation: Due to this misdeed, you, sullied by my wrath, will reside in the human world for some time.

verses.sanskrit: बुधस्य पुत्रो राजर्षिः काशीराजः पुरूरवाः । तमद्य गच्छ दुर्बुद्धे स ते भर्ता भविष्यति ।। 7.56.25 ।।
verses.translation: Go today to Pururavas, the royal sage, son of Budha and king of Kashi, O ill-minded one, for he will be your husband.

verses.sanskrit: ततः सा शापदोषेण पुरूरवसमभ्यगात् । प्रतियाते पुरूरवं बुधस्यात्मजमौरसम् ।। 7.56.26 ।।
verses.translation: Then she, by the effect of the curse, went to Pururavas, the legitimate son of Budha, who had returned.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य जज्ञे ततः श्रीमानायुः पुत्रो महाबलः । नहुषो यस्य पुत्रस्तु बभूवेन्द्रसमद्युतिः ।। 7.56.27 ।।
verses.translation: Then to him was born a glorious and very powerful son named Ayus, whose son, Nahusha, became one with splendor equal to Indra.

verses.sanskrit: वज्रमुत्सृज्य वृत्राय भ्रान्ते ऽथ त्रिदिवेश्वरे । शतं वर्षसहस्राणि येनेन्द्रत्वं प्रशासितम् ।। 7.56.28 ।।
verses.translation: This refers to the one who ruled as Indra for a hundred thousand years when the actual Indra was agitated after casting his thunderbolt at Vritra.

verses.sanskrit: सा तेन शापेन जगाम भूमिं तदोर्वशी चारुदती सुनेत्रा । बहूनि वर्षाण्यवसच्च सुभ्रूः शापक्षयादिन्द्रसदो ययौ च ।। 7.56.29 ।।
verses.translation: That Urvashi, with beautiful teeth, eyes, and eyebrows, then went to the earth due to that curse, resided there for many years, and returned to Indra's assembly upon the curse's expiration. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे षट्पञ्चाशः सर्गः ।। 56 ।। Thus ends the fifty-sixth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
